skill.name: backend-queries
skill.description: Guides secure and performant backend database query work, including parameterized queries, eager loading, indexing, pagination, caching, and transactions.
skill.summary: Improve backend database query security, performance, and maintainability.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static path traversal findings are confirmed as a real parent-directory reference in SKILL.md, but the context is a documentation link rather than executable code. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at lines 3 and 8 are false positives from normal prose, with no evidence of cryptographic implementation or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 28 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/queries.md. This directs the AI agent to read a file outside the skill package boundary, which can cause unexpected local filesystem access and makes the skill depend on unavailable external context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal sequence is directly present in the referenced file. The context is a Markdown documentation link, so the risk is bounded and not evidence of malicious execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is Benign
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 occur in ordinary descriptive text. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, encryption code, hashing code, or cryptographic configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about backend query guidance. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptographic usage in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal Sequence
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md line 28 contains ../../../ in a link target. Even when used for documentation, this pattern can lead an AI agent outside the packaged skill directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is explicit at the cited line. The absence of scripts or command execution lowers the severity.

content.value_statement: Backend query code often grows slow, unsafe, or hard to maintain as features expand. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward parameterized, efficient, and transaction-safe database query patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of parameterized queries for safer database access.
Recommends eager loading to reduce N+1 query problems.
Promotes selecting only needed columns instead of broad result sets.
Supports filtering, sorting, pagination, joins, subqueries, and aggregation guidance.
Encourages transactions for related database writes.
Covers query performance topics such as indexes, caching, and timeouts.
limitations: The skill references an external standards file outside the package.
It provides guidance only and does not run database tests.
It does not inspect live schemas, indexes, or production query plans by itself.
It does not include framework-specific helper code in the reviewed package.

use_cases.title: Harden Repository Queries
use_cases.description: Review repository methods for unsafe raw SQL, missing parameterization, or overly broad selections.

use_cases.title: Optimize API List Endpoints
use_cases.description: Design filtering, sorting, and pagination that keeps list endpoints fast and predictable.

use_cases.title: Plan Transaction Boundaries
use_cases.description: Identify related write operations that should be wrapped in a database transaction.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Simple Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one backend query.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend query for security and performance. Check parameterization, selected columns, and simple index needs.

prompt_templates.title: Fix an N+1 Problem
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate optimization of ORM loading behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find potential N+1 query problems in this controller or service. Suggest eager loading changes and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Pagination and Filtering
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced endpoint design for list queries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design safe filtering, sorting, and pagination for this list endpoint. Include validation rules and performance concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Transaction Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of related writes and consistency requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this multi-step database workflow. Identify transaction boundaries, rollback behavior, locking concerns, and timeout risks.

output_examples.input: A controller loads users and then loads each user's orders inside a loop.
output: Flags the repeated order lookup as an N+1 query risk.
Suggests eager loading orders with the initial user query.
Notes that selected columns should be limited to fields used by the response.

output_examples.input: A service builds a raw search query from request parameters.
output: Identifies unsafe query construction as a parameterization issue.
Recommends binding search values instead of concatenating input.
Suggests validating allowed sort fields before building the query.

output_examples.input: A checkout flow creates several related database records.
output: Recommends a transaction around the related writes.
Calls out rollback needs for partial failure.
Suggests checking timeout and locking behavior for high-traffic paths.

best_practices: Provide the model with relevant schema details, indexes, and expected result sizes.
Ask for security and performance review together, because query changes often affect both.
Verify suggested query changes with tests, explain plans, or real performance measurements.
anti_patterns: Do not accept raw SQL changes without checking parameter binding.
Do not optimize by adding eager loading without confirming response needs.
Do not add caching before defining invalidation and freshness requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill write database queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the AI tool when writing or reviewing backend database query code.

faq.question: Does it support ORMs and query builders?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers query builders, ORM methods, eager loading, joins, and related backend query patterns.

faq.question: Can it prevent SQL injection?
faq.answer: It promotes parameterized queries and safer raw SQL patterns, but final security depends on implementation and testing.

faq.question: Can it optimize slow database queries automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest improvements, but users should validate changes with measurements and database explain plans.

faq.question: Why is there a marketplace warning?
faq.answer: The reviewed skill links to a parent-directory file outside the packaged skill, which can cause unexpected local file access.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
